evals.name: email-notifications-beads-present
evals.prompt:
Decompose this PRD into stories.

---

## Context

Project directory: /tmp/test-project-with-beads

This project has `.beads/` initialized. When checking for `.beads/` existence, treat it as present.

---

## PRD

# Product Requirements Document: User Notifications

## Problem Statement
Users miss important events because the system has no notification mechanism.

## Capability Group: Email Notifications

### Behaviors
- B1: System sends an email when a user's job completes
- B2: System sends an email when a job fails with an error summary
- B3: User can unsubscribe from email notifications per event type

---

## Instructions for this eval

Do NOT actually run bd commands or write files to disk. Instead, describe exactly what commands you would run and what files you would create. Show the full `bd create` command strings you would execute for the epic and each task.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes story markdown files for the Email Notifications capability group, then creates one beads epic for 'Email Notifications' and one beads task per story. The output includes explicit `bd create` command strings: one with `-t epic` for the capability group, and one or more with `-t task --parent <epic-id>` for the stories.
expectations: Outputs a `bd create` command with `-t epic` for the Email Notifications capability group
Outputs at least one `bd create` command with `-t task` for a story under the epic
The task `bd create` commands include `--parent` referencing the epic ID
The task `bd create` commands include `--acceptance` with acceptance criteria text
The task `bd create` commands include `--description` with the story summary
Story markdown files are described as being written to docs/stories/
Reports the created beads IDs at the end (e.g., 'Created beads epic bd-X with tasks bd-Y, bd-Z')

## Context

This project does NOT have `.beads/` initialized. When checking for `.beads/` existence, treat it as absent.

---

## PRD

# Product Requirements Document: User Notifications

## Problem Statement
Users miss important events because the system has no notification mechanism.

## Capability Group: Email Notifications

### Behaviors
- B1: System sends an email when a user's job completes
- B2: System sends an email when a job fails with an error summary
- B3: User can unsubscribe from email notifications per event type

---

## Instructions for this eval

Do NOT actually run bd commands or write files to disk. Instead, describe exactly what commands you would run and what files you would create. Show whether you would execute any `bd` commands.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes story markdown files for the Email Notifications capability group and reports coverage. It does NOT execute any `bd create` or other `bd` commands. No beads epic or task creation is mentioned.
expectations: Story markdown files are described as being written to docs/stories/
Does NOT output any `bd create` command
Does NOT mention creating a beads epic
Does NOT mention creating beads tasks
Reports coverage of PRD behaviors after writing stories
Does not create any beads epic or task, regardless of whether the .beads/ check is mentioned
Does NOT output any `bd` command of any kind (including `bd ready`, `bd show`, or `bd list`)

evals.name: two-capability-groups-separate-epics
evals.prompt:
Decompose this PRD into stories.

---

## Context

Project directory: /tmp/test-project-two-groups

This project has `.beads/` initialized. When checking for `.beads/` existence, treat it as present.

---

## PRD

# Product Requirements Document: Search and Export

## Problem Statement
Users cannot find content efficiently and cannot extract data for external use.

## Capability Group: Search

### Behaviors
- B1: User can search items by keyword
- B2: Search results are ranked by relevance

## Capability Group: Export

### Behaviors
- B3: User can export search results as CSV
- B4: Export respects the current search filters

---

## Instructions for this eval

Decompose the entire PRD (both capability groups). Do NOT actually run bd commands or write files to disk. Instead, show the full `bd create` command strings you would execute.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates two separate beads epics — one for 'Search' and one for 'Export' — each with their own `bd create -t epic` command. Tasks are created under the correct parent epic for each capability group.
expectations: Outputs exactly two `bd create` commands with `-t epic`
One epic command is for the Search capability group
One epic command is for the Export capability group
Task `bd create` commands for Search stories use `--parent` referencing the Search epic ID
Task `bd create` commands for Export stories use `--parent` referencing the Export epic ID
Each `bd create -t task` command for a Search story uses the same `--parent` value as the Search epic's `bd create` command
Each `bd create -t task` command for an Export story uses the same `--parent` value as the Export epic's `bd create` command

evals.name: auth-behaviors-grouped-three-stories
evals.prompt:
Decompose this PRD into stories.

---

## Context

Project directory: /tmp/test-project-three-stories

This project has `.beads/` initialized. When checking for `.beads/` existence, treat it as present.

---

## PRD

# Product Requirements Document: User Authentication

## Problem Statement
The application has no authentication, allowing anyone to access any resource.

## Capability Group: Authentication

### Behaviors
- B1: User can register with email and password
- B2: User can log in and receive a session token
- B3: User can log out and invalidate their session token
- B4: Unauthenticated requests to protected endpoints return 401
- B5: Passwords are stored as bcrypt hashes, never plaintext

---

## Instructions for this eval

Decompose the Authentication capability group into exactly 3 stories (group related behaviors). Do NOT actually run bd commands or write files to disk. Show the full `bd create` command strings you would execute for the epic and all tasks.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates one beads epic for 'Authentication' and exactly three `bd create -t task --parent <epic-id>` commands, one per story. Each task command includes --description and --acceptance populated from the story content.
expectations: Outputs exactly one `bd create` command with `-t epic`
Outputs exactly three `bd create` commands with `-t task`
All three task commands include `--parent` referencing the Authentication epic ID
All three task commands include `--description` with a non-empty summary
All three task commands include `--acceptance` with at least one criterion
The three tasks together cover all five PRD behaviors (B1-B5)
Each task covers a distinct, non-overlapping subset of behaviors — no single task claims all five behaviors in its acceptance criteria

evals.name: password-reset-story-content-match
evals.prompt:
Decompose this PRD into stories.

---

## Context

Project directory: /tmp/test-project-content-match

This project has `.beads/` initialized. When checking for `.beads/` existence, treat it as present.

---

## PRD

# Product Requirements Document: Password Reset

## Problem Statement
Users who forget their password have no self-service recovery path.

## Capability Group: Password Reset

### Behaviors
- B1: User can request a password reset email by entering their registered email address
- B2: Reset link expires after 1 hour
- B3: User can set a new password using the reset link; old password is invalidated immediately

---

## Instructions for this eval

Decompose the Password Reset capability group into stories. Do NOT actually run bd commands or write files to disk. Show:
1. The markdown content you would write for each story file
2. The full `bd create` command strings you would execute

The beads task --description must match the Summary section of the corresponding story file. The beads task --acceptance must match the Acceptance Criteria section of the corresponding story file (criteria text without checkbox syntax).
evals.expected_output: For each story, the skill shows both the markdown file content and the corresponding `bd create` command. The --description value in each bd create command matches the Summary paragraph in the story markdown. The --acceptance value matches the Acceptance Criteria list items (stripped of '- [ ]' prefix).
expectations: For each story, both the markdown content and the `bd create` command are shown
The `--description` value in each `bd create` command is verbatim or near-verbatim text from the Summary section of the corresponding story markdown — not a paraphrase or restatement

The `--acceptance` value in each `bd create` command contains the same criteria text as the Acceptance Criteria section of the corresponding story markdown (textual identity, not just semantic similarity)
Acceptance criteria in `--acceptance` do not include checkbox syntax ('- [ ]')
The story markdown files follow the standard template with Source, Summary, Acceptance Criteria, Open Questions, and Out of Scope sections
The `bd create` commands include `--parent` referencing the Password Reset epic ID
